text: A microcontroller is like a small conductor on a tiny podium (chip): it receives power (positive and negative) and raises or lowers its hands (pins) with 3 V — sometimes "HIGH", sometimes "LOW". This way it controls an entire orchestra of devices, exactly according to the score (program in C++), which tells it when and how to give the signals!

Great mnemonic! The microcontroller as a conductor, supplied with power and raising or lowering its "hands" (pins) (3 V "HIGH" or "LOW"), thus controls devices according to a program (C++), exactly as described in the learning material.
